scope: one jacket, one five-edit chain per editor, no replicates or usable seeds
supported_findings: After one naive full-frame edit, protected-label byte exactness is 0.619369% for gpt-image-2 and 0.281532% for nano-banana-pro.
The ledger label is 100% byte-exact for both editors because pixels outside each declared support are copied forward.
Gate v1 commits 4/5 gpt-image-2 candidates and 3/5 nano-banana-pro candidates; the latter has two below-threshold target-change rejections.

manual_visual_audit.observation: Nano-banana-pro keeps the label spatially aligned and has higher SSIM, yet its letterforms visibly diverge from the original across the chain. Gpt-image-2 moves and eventually clips the label in this fixed crop.
manual_visual_audit.metric_implication: SSIM alone cannot certify text identity. OCR would be a necessary-not-sufficient check: misspelling can fail, but correct spelling cannot certify typography.
manual_visual_audit.not_claimed: No OCR score, independent human rating, or general model ranking.

limitations: The editors are non-deterministic and were each sampled once. Saved broad-region metrics are excluded here because rejected-turn union bookkeeping differed in the historical receipts. Cost values are configured estimates, not per-run invoices.
